claim: Later team progress is preserved as additive queue-update records rather than by overwriting the canonical queue snapshot.
observed: The shared coordination rule limits teams to owned records.
The canonical queue blob predates later additive queue-update commits.
M-006 explicitly records canonical_mutation_performed false and states that its additive update does not overwrite ACTIVE_QUEUE.json.
inferred: The repository currently behaves as an append-oriented queue state log.
proposed: Create a deterministic provenance-preserving reducer for baseline plus additive queue updates.
superseded: Treating ACTIVE_QUEUE.json alone as the complete current queue state.
unresolved: Conflict ordering and field-level merge semantics for incompatible updates to one queue item.
verification: CM-1003 was created successfully at commit 726d749336ddde9f5e0d115499eed29c77ecf007. This proves source commitment, not execution of a reducer or corpus test.
privacy_boundary: Public repository files and commit metadata only; no private chat, health, credentials, identifying personal content, or unreviewed uploads.
falsification_route: Inspect the exact blobs and commit; reject the claim if later progress is authoritatively folded into ACTIVE_QUEUE.json or if an existing reducer already defines authoritative ordering and conflict resolution.
